@@ -269,7 +269,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-nk6u9","title":"[MEDIUM] simd_scan ansi_byte_count misses OSC/DCS/PM/APC string bodies (terminates on first letter after ESC)","description":"# Defect

## Concrete failure path

`scan_newlines_and_ansi_*` (4 variants in `crates/frankenterm-core/src/simd_scan.rs`) treats any ASCII letter (0x40..=0x7E, excluding `[`) immediately after ESC as the terminator of the escape sequence. That rule is correct for CSI (`ESC [ … letter`) and for single-char C1 controls like `ESC M` (reverse index). It is **wrong** for the five C1 introducers that open a *string* sequence:

| Intro byte | Sequence | Continues until |
|------------|----------|-----------------|
| `]` (0x5D) | OSC (Operating System Command — window titles, clipboard, hyperlinks) | ST (`ESC \\`) or BEL (0x07) |
| `P` (0x50) | DCS (Device Control String — e.g., Sixel graphics, DECRQSS replies) | ST |
| `^` (0x5E) | PM (Privacy Message) | ST |
| `_` (0x5F) | APC (Application Program Command — e.g., Kitty graphics) | ST |
| `X` (0x58) | SOS (Start Of String) | ST |

All five land in the `0x40..=0x7E && != '['` branch and prematurely exit the escape. The string body + ST/BEL bytes are then counted as **plain text**, not ANSI.

## Reproducer (currently NO test covers this)

```rust
let data = b"\x1b]0;window title\x07after";
let scan = scan_newlines_and_ansi(data);
// Current:  ansi_byte_count = 2  (just ESC + ])
// Correct:  ansi_byte_count = 19 (ESC + ] + "0;window title" + BEL)
assert_eq!(scan.ansi_byte_count, 19);
```

Same bug in the `_with_state` variants — the cross-chunk state carries only `in_escape: bool` with no notion of "string mode," so the first chunk's body bytes leak into the "non-ANSI" count.

## Blast radius

- `ansi_density` (`OutputScanMetrics::ansi_density`) underreports on panes that emit OSC/DCS/PM/APC/SOS sequences — common in production: window-title updates from shell prompts, terminfo hyperlinks, Sixel graphics, Kitty image protocol, DCS replies to DECRQSS probes.
- `ScanMetricsSummary` is consumed by compression and backpressure decision paths; an underreport makes the system think a pane is producing plain text when it is actually producing high-ANSI output.
- Not a crash, not data loss — silent wrong answer in the metrics.

## Fix plan

1. Extend `OutputScanState` with a `StringPhase` — `InString` and `InStringSawEsc` so the stateful scanner can resume a partial `ESC \\` terminator across chunk boundaries.
2. After ESC is seen, if the next byte is one of \{0x50, 0x58, 0x5D, 0x5E, 0x5F\}, transition to `InString` instead of clearing `in_escape`.
3. While in `InString`: count every byte as ANSI; terminate on BEL (0x07) or ESC \\ (0x1b 0x5c).
4. Patch all four scan functions (memchr + scalar, stateful + non-stateful).
5. Add targeted regression tests: basic OSC, OSC + ST, OSC + BEL, DCS, PM, APC, chunk split through the body, chunk split on ESC-\\, and OSC containing embedded newlines (verify newline_count is unaffected).

Scope: single file (`simd_scan.rs`). No public-API break; `OutputScanState` gains a field but `Default` still returns the "no carry" state.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:07:00.334737Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:13:27.862859Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:13:27.862491Z","close_reason":"Landed in 99703277 (feat(simd_scan): add string-phase state machine for OSC/DCS/SOS/PM/APC byte counting)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-nnfwn","title":"[HIGH] MCP no-db manifest test does not actually freeze the advertised no-db surface","description":"Commit bba76a57 claims complete MCP manifest freezing, but `mcp_manifest_matches_golden_without_db` only checks that the tool array is non-empty, that names have a rough prefix shape, and that the JSON round-trips (`crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_manifest_golden.rs:155-178`). It does not compare the no-db tool list against a golden, does not assert the promised strict-subset relation, and does not validate no-db resources or resource templates at all. Result: a public MCP surface regression in no-db mode can ship silently as long as tool names still look plausible. Expected fix: freeze or structurally compare the no-db tools/resources/templates against a committed expectation and explicitly assert that db-gated entries are absent.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T05:57:00.488118Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T06:24:29.244984Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T06:24:29.244768Z","close_reason":"No-db MCP manifest now frozen and subset-checked","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-npj7t","title":"[tx][compensation] PaneStepExecutor compensation path returns synthetic success without executing rollback actions","description":"In `crates/frankenterm-core/src/tx_execution.rs:633-673`, `PaneStepExecutor::execute_compensations()` has no access to the contract compensation plan, leaves `contract_compensations` empty, and returns `success: true` / `reason_code: "compensation_succeeded"` for each committed step unless a deterministic injected failure was requested. The comments explicitly admit this is synthetic reporting rather than real rollback.

Impact: tx rollback/compensation can report success even when no compensation action actually ran. That is a production-path false positive in the tx data plane.

Fix direction: thread the contract compensation actions into the executor API or execution engine so compensation dispatch can execute real `StepAction`s rather than emitting synthetic success records.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:04:05.868290Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:12:30.633077Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T20:12:30.631962Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 74a775bc: thread contract compensations into PaneStepExecutor and execute real rollback actions","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"ft-nrbrp","title":"[HIGH] runtime recv_event collapses Cancelled into channel-close and can drop queued capture events","description":"Review finding in crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime.rs:216-217 and 2956-2990. The local helper `recv_event(runtime_cx, rx)` is `rx.recv(runtime_cx).await.ok()`, so it converts every receive error into `None`. Under runtime_compat, `mpsc::Receiver::recv(cx)` returns `Err(RecvError::Cancelled)` for a pre-cancelled cx. The capture relay loop treats `None` as end-of-stream and immediately `break`s, then closes `capture_ring_tx`. Repro: create a capture-ingress mpsc with queued events, pre-cancel the loop cx before the next `recv_event`, keep `shutdown_flag == false`; `recv_event` returns `None`, the relay exits, and buffered capture events are abandoned even though the ingress channel was not closed. This conflates cancellation with sender disconnect and can turn task-graph cancellation ordering into silent ingest data loss. Expected: preserve the error distinction so cancellation does not masquerade as channel closure; either retry/drain under shutdown policy or propagate an explicit cancelled reason instead of closing the ring as if producers were gone.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T18:19:33.517921Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T18:19:33.517921Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ingest","review"]}
+{"id":"ft-nrbrp","title":"[HIGH] runtime recv_event collapses Cancelled into channel-close and can drop queued capture events","description":"Review finding in crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime.rs:216-217 and 2956-2990. The local helper `recv_event(runtime_cx, rx)` is `rx.recv(runtime_cx).await.ok()`, so it converts every receive error into `None`. Under runtime_compat, `mpsc::Receiver::recv(cx)` returns `Err(RecvError::Cancelled)` for a pre-cancelled cx. The capture relay loop treats `None` as end-of-stream and immediately `break`s, then closes `capture_ring_tx`. Repro: create a capture-ingress mpsc with queued events, pre-cancel the loop cx before the next `recv_event`, keep `shutdown_flag == false`; `recv_event` returns `None`, the relay exits, and buffered capture events are abandoned even though the ingress channel was not closed. This conflates cancellation with sender disconnect and can turn task-graph cancellation ordering into silent ingest data loss. Expected: preserve the error distinction so cancellation does not masquerade as channel closure; either retry/drain under shutdown policy or propagate an explicit cancelled reason instead of closing the ring as if producers were gone.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T18:19:33.517921Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T18:33:29.495488Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T18:33:29.495226Z","close_reason":"Fixed recv_event cancellation semantics; Cancelled no longer masquerades as channel close","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ingest","review"]}

 {"id":"ft-o4xi3","title":"Extend proptest_mcp + proptest_mcp_bridge structural invariants","description":"proptest_mcp.rs (3.7KB) and proptest_mcp_bridge.rs (4.0KB) are thin. Add structural protocol invariants: all tool names follow 'wa.' or 'frankenterm.' convention, all resource URIs use a wa:// scheme, all resource templates contain {} placeholders, tool/resource/template sets are stable across rebuilds, initialize response is deterministic for the same config, and the client-side tool list matches the server for multiple suffixes simultaneously. Complements the ft-mejto golden without duplicating it.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T05:38:12.628263Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T06:06:42.089459Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T06:06:42.089037Z","close_reason":"proptest_mcp_bridge extended with 5 new structural invariants (wa.* namespace, wa:// scheme, resource/template disjointness, build idempotency, non-empty descriptions). proptest_mcp.rs left untouched due to pre-existing test-binary flakiness with >3 proptests (server threads leak).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mcp","proptest","testing"]}

 {"id":"ft-u4m4z","title":"[patterns] gemini.session.resume_hint lacks near-miss corpus freeze for resume anchor","description":"Rule gemini.session.resume_hint in crates/frankenterm-core/src/patterns.rs matches broad 'gemini resume' / 'gemini --resume' text and currently only has positive corpus fixtures (session_resume_hint.txt + session_resume_hint_v2.txt). Add a negative near-miss corpus pair to prevent false positives on docs/help text or malformed session IDs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:27:26.597674Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:32:09.604955Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:32:09.604621Z","close_reason":"Near-miss corpus added in f684c840: session_resume_hint_near_miss.{txt,expect.json} with six realistic false-positive candidates (help-text <session-id> placeholder, short-id 'abc', quoted docs backtick form, gemini-cli variant, single-quoted reference, flag form followed by non-hex word). Each line has a specific reason why either the anchor or the regex at patterns.rs:2067-2077 fails; expect.json is []. Verified locally with a Python regex equivalent: global search returns None, per-line anchor/regex trace shows every anchor-matching line is regex=False. Matches the existing near-miss pattern established by ft-8ku8p (model_used) and the gemini oauth/rate-limit/session_summary negatives.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-uigpj","title":"diagnostic.rs: DiagnosticOptions struct literals missing include_full_cwd (2 sites)","description":"diagnostic.rs:1817 and :2711 both construct DiagnosticOptions via struct literal but omit the include_full_cwd field that was added to the struct definition.  {"id":"ft-uj5c6","title":"patterns.rs dedup compile break blocks qiba0 MCP golden harness","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T07:04:07.653677Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T07:22:43.810694Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T07:22:43.810041Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 5dcea11e","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-uoh4e","title":"wa.wait_for and wa.send accept unbounded timeout_secs","description":"MCP wa.wait_for and wa.send enforce timeout_secs >= 1 but do not enforce the max bound used by adjacent cass MCP tools. Because these handlers block on a current-thread runtime while waiting for pane output, a malicious or buggy MCP client can hold the handler thread for arbitrarily long durations. Fix by adding a schema maximum and server-side guard for both tools, with regression tests.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"RoseCat","created_at":"2026-04-24T18:31:37.288728Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T18:31:37.288728Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mcp","review","safety"]}
 {"id":"ft-uokgq","title":"[review][MEDIUM] release.yml Windows smoke test silently swallows native command failures","description":"commit 2f25ea5d added a Windows smoke test step using `shell: pwsh` at .github/workflows/release.yml:82-96. The script invokes the ft.exe binary via the PowerShell call operator (`& $binary --version`), but does NOT configure PowerShell to fail on native command errors:

    - name: Smoke test binary (Windows)
      if: runner.os == 'Windows'
      shell: pwsh
      run: |
        $binary = "target/${{ matrix.target }}/release/${{ matrix.artifact }}"
        Write-Host "=== ft --version ==="
        & $binary --version
        ...
        & $binary doctor --json
        Write-Host "=== All smoke tests passed ==="

GitHub Actions default pwsh invocation is `pwsh -command ". '{0}'"` — no `-ErrorActionPreference Stop`, no `$PSNativeCommandUseErrorActionPreference`. When a native command like `ft doctor --json` exits non-zero:
- PowerShell sets `$LASTEXITCODE` but does NOT throw.
- The script continues past the failing command.
- The last statement is `Write-Host '=== All smoke tests passed ==='` which exits 0.
- The step's final exit code is 0 → GitHub Actions reports success.

Effect: a Windows-only regression where ft.exe panics at startup, crashes on --help, or exits non-zero from doctor would ship to release WITHOUT the workflow catching it. The whole point of the ft-ych74 fix was to close this gap for Windows — and this silently defeats it.

The Unix counterpart at :68-80 (`shell: bash` default) uses GitHub Actions' default args `bash --noprofile --norc -eo pipefail {0}` → `set -e` is implicit, any non-zero exit aborts the step immediately.

Fix (add at top of pwsh script block):

    $ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
    $PSNativeCommandUseErrorActionPreference = $true

`$PSNativeCommandUseErrorActionPreference` (PowerShell 7.3+) makes native command failures throw terminating errors under $ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'. pwsh on ubuntu-latest / windows-latest is 7.4+, so this is safe to enable.

Alternative: check `$LASTEXITCODE` after each `& $binary ...` call and `throw` explicitly.

Severity: MEDIUM. Defeats the intent of 2f25ea5d (which closed my earlier bead ft-ych74). Discovered during reviewer audit round 9, 2026-04-23.

Tag: review, release, ci, windows, smoke-test.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:54:20.675627Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:12:14.182966Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:12:14.179986Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 89ead0c9. Windows pwsh smoke step now sets $ErrorActionPreference='Stop' and $PSNativeCommandUseErrorActionPreference=$true at the top of the block, so any non-zero exit from ft.exe (from --version / version --full / --help / doctor --json) now raises a terminating error and fails the step — closing the fail-open that defeated ft-ych74's intent. PowerShell 7.3+ feature; Windows GHA runners ship pwsh 7.4+. Could not validate locally (no pwsh installed) and did not dispatch the workflow since that would trigger an actual 5-platform release build; next tag push will exercise the path.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}
 {"id":"ft-up6u2","title":"[security] mission + tx contract loaders lack size cap — OOM vector for hostile repo .ft/mission/*.json","description":"# Bug

`mcp_load_mission_from_path` (mcp_missions.rs:257) and
`mcp_load_mission_tx_contract_from_path` (mcp_missions.rs:141) both
use bare `std::fs::read_to_string(path)` with no stat-before-read
size guard.

Pattern-pack loader has this exact guard (ft-05hfm, MAX_PACK_BYTES =
16 MiB). Mission files under `.ft/mission/*.json` have the same
trust property as patterns under `.ft/patterns/*.{yaml,json,toml}` —
a hostile repo can ship them, and they're loaded at MCP tool
invocation time.

# Attack

1. Hostile repo ships `.ft/mission/active.json` = 5 GiB of garbage.
2. Operator (or agent via wa.mission_state MCP) opens the project.
3. `wa.mission_state` → `mcp_load_mission_from_path` →
   `read_to_string` inhales 5 GiB into memory → watcher OOMs.

Same attack for `.ft/mission/tx-active.json` via wa.tx_show / wa.tx_run.

`resolve_workspace_scoped_path` (mcp_missions.rs:61) blocks
path-traversal but does NOT cap file size inside the workspace.

# Fix

Add `MAX_MISSION_BYTES` + `MAX_TX_CONTRACT_BYTES` constants (16 MiB
each, matching MAX_PACK_BYTES) with a stat-before-read check + a
second-line check after read, mirroring the pattern-pack loader
structure.

Both constants at 16 MiB because:
- mission JSON + 100 assignments ≈ 50-100 KiB realistically
- tx contract + plan ≈ similar
- 16 MiB = 200x headroom

# Tests

Regression tests similar to `load_pack_from_file_rejects_oversize_yaml`:
  - `mcp_load_mission_from_path_rejects_oversize_json`
  - `mcp_load_mission_tx_contract_from_path_rejects_oversize_json`","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:25:19.149935Z","created_by":"pane3-mission-cap","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:45:21.408462Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:45:21.408112Z","close_reason":"Landed in 3e2ae80c. Added MAX_MISSION_BYTES and MAX_TX_CONTRACT_BYTES (both 16 MiB, matching MAX_PACK_BYTES from ft-05hfm) + shared read_capped_file helper + 2 regression tests (load_tx_contract_rejects_oversize_json, load_mission_rejects_oversize_json). Preserved historical robot.tx_not_found / robot.mission_not_found error codes on NotFound branch. Full verification blocked on ft-golgl (pre-existing compile errors in diagnostic.rs, outcome.rs, workflows/handlers.rs under --features mcp); changes reviewed against the ft-05hfm pattern shipping on HEAD.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mcp","mission","security"]}
 {"id":"ft-uqlrq","title":"[mock] search/model2vec_embedder.rs:36 embed() is a stub that always errors","description":"search/model2vec_embedder.rs:36-38 has `fn embed(&self, _text: &str) -> Result<Vec<f32>, EmbedError> { // Stub: real implementation loads ONNX model; Err(EmbedError::ModelNotFound(...)) }`. The entire Embedder trait impl is a stub that always returns ModelNotFound. Zero production callers exist — tests in search/mod.rs and daemon/worker.rs explicitly verify model2vec is "retired" and rejected. Production behavior should be: either (a) implement real ONNX model loading if model2vec is still planned, or (b) delete the file entirely since it is dead code with no callers and tests actively reject it.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:32:43.678300Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:52:17.389463Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:52:17.389208Z","close_reason":"Shipped in af925d26. Audit confirmed the file was orphaned:
- not declared in search/mod.rs (no 'mod model2vec_embedder;' anywhere)
- zero constructors of Model2VecEmbedder outside the file itself
- three tests across the tree explicitly verify model2vec is RETIRED (never advertised as a tier, rejected as a selector, rejected by the embed worker): search::tests::advertised_embedder_tiers_never_reports_retired_model2vec_backend, search::daemon::worker::tests::build_embedder_rejects_retired_model2vec_selector, tests/semantic_backend_selection_integration.rs::embed_worker_rejects_retired_model2vec_selector

Decision rationale: the bead's two options (a/b) are 'wire up real ONNX impl' or 'surface via feature flag + clear error'. Both miss for orphan code with retirement tests in place:
- (a) would contradict those tests and re-expand a surface we deliberately shrank
- (b) only helps if there's a live caller who needs a clear error; there isn't one

The correct 'not supported in this build' signal for orphan code is absence. Delete the 40-line skeleton; a future model2vec revival will write fresh against whatever the Embedder trait looks like then. Also updated the stale 'e.g. Model2Vec distilled' comment on ManagedEmbedderStack.fast in orchestrator.rs to describe the Fast-tier slot generically + cross-link the retirement test.

The three retirement tests stay intact — they continue pinning the invariant.

Verification (rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-p5-orch-target):
- cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --no-default-features: clean
- --features semantic-search build blocked by 13 pre-existing unrelated errors in web/sse.rs (select! macro fallout from the 9e1bd63b cfg-strip); my patch only removes a file + edits a doc comment so it cannot introduce compile errors

Note: the commit that carries the change is misleadingly titled 'docs(readme): correct runtime_compat boundary claim' — another agent's linter bundled my uncommitted model2vec deletion + orchestrator comment into its own commit. The 44 lines of change are correct (40 deletions in model2vec_embedder.rs + 4 lines in orchestrator.rs doc comment); only the commit title under-describes the scope.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
